sglang.title: Create col-major and tma-aligned x_scale for deep_gemm.gemm_fp8_fp8_bf16_nt
sglang.author: strgrb
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
deep_gemm.gemm_fp8_fp8_bf16_nt requires a col-major and tma-aligned x_scale for better performance, by avoiding copy in get_col_major_tma_aligned_tensor. We can manage it directly in per_token_group_quant_fp8
Modifications

update deep_gemm commit with this pr: deepseek-ai/DeepGEMM#65
add

sglang.title: [Feature] Integrate DeepEP into SGLang
sglang.author: liz-badada
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

Intergrate DeepEP into SGLang framework. Still WIP but could use '--enable-dp-attention --enable-deepep-moe' to trigger DeepEP intranode / internode, please follow the install guide of NVSHMEM dependency, also provide a Dockerfile.deepep based on SGLang image.
Co-auther @xutizhou
Note:

sglang.title: Support FlashMLA backend cuda graph

Motivation
Support FlashMLA backend cuda graph. Optimize index calculation, complete the calculation in init_forward
Modifications

Optimize the FlashInfer block table calculation logic to compute only once during the forward pass.
Support FlashMLA backend CUDA Graph.
Automatically set page=64 when

sglang.title: Add clang-format to pre-commit config
sglang.author: Hongbosherlock
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
follow: #4194 and #4203
There is clang-format check in pr-test-sgl-kernel.yml now.  If the local code format doesn't meet the requirements, the CI check fails
To avoid manually running clang-format before each commit and to ensure code style consistency with the project, I added clang-for

sglang.title: [fix] fix initialization of _ENABLE_TORCH_INFERENCE_MODE
sglang.author: Alcanderian
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
fix  #4372 (comment)

Modifications
Move the declaration of get_bool_env_var before _ENABLE_TORCH_INFERENCE_MODE.

Checklist

 Format your code according to the Code Formatting with Pre-Commit.

sglang.title: avoid cudaStreamSynchronize in DeepSeekV2AttentionMLA
sglang.author: strgrb
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

I profiled deepseek and observed some bubbles in timeline, finally found the cause:

This is caused by D2H copy in DeepSeekV2AttentionMLA.
Modifications

Using forward_batch.extend_prefix_lens_cpu directy instead of forward_batch.extend_prefix_lens, this can decrease TTFT
Checklist

 For

sglang.title: Support `n` in OpenAI API completions
sglang.author: ChuyueSun
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Add support for n in OpenAI API to support multiple completions sampling. It is different from fork(n).
Modifications
Use example: openai_completion_n.py
Format change:

generate returns a str (n=1) or list[str].
openai_completion  returns a str (n=1) or list[str].
Without specifying n, n

vllm.title: [V1][Bugfix] Fix oracle for device checking
vllm.author: ywang96
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Only AMD & TPU are experimental support on V1, and all other devices will simply error out on V1, so this PR adds this logic explicitly.
FIXES #15057

vllm.title: [Misc] Avoid unnecessary HF `do_rescale` warning when passing dummy data
vllm.author: DarkLight1337

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix size calculation of processing cache
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The processing cache incorrectly calculated the item size as basically zero because it failed to recurse into the individual tensors, resulting in effectively infinite capacity.
FIX #15105
FIX #15085

vllm.author: MarcCote

This PR updates the following tip info under the Use vLLM’s Official Docker Image section of the docs.

There is no need for --system when updating the Python installation is already managed by uv (according to this astral-sh/uv#12204 (comment)).
When running with --system I was getting this error:

vllm.title: [Misc][Benchmark] Add support for different `tokenizer_mode`
vllm.author: aarnphm
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR adds support to tokenizer_mode in sampling example requests for supporting running this benchmark with Mistral models.
tokenizer_mode=auto doesn't seem to detect mistral tokenizer correctly (which is possibly a bug. However, the Mistral team suggest to set tokenizer_mode explicitly regardles

vllm.title: [Doc] Update the "the first vLLM China Meetup" slides link to point to the first page

vllm.bodyExcerpt:
In README.md, the link to the slides for "the First vLLM China Meetup" points to page 35 instead of page 1, which may be a bit confusing.
This PR replaces that link with one that points to page 1 instead.

vllm.title: [V1][TPU] Change kv cache shape.
vllm.author: vanbasten23
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR changes the kv cache shape from [num_blocks, block_size, num_kv_heads, head_size] to [num_blocks, block_size, num_kv_heads * head_size], in accordance with the ragged paged attention kernel change pytorch/xla#8851, in order to unblock the multi-chip scenario:

before the change, the ragged p

vllm.title: [BugFix] Lazily import XgrammarBackend to avoid early cuda init
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Importing xgrammar appears to initialize the cuda context, which we don't want to do in the front-end process. It also means that the server can't be started with the (default) multiproc context mode of fork.
I guess this is what LazyLoader is meant to help with, but it doesn't seem to be working as
